steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text introduces ancient DNA (the established approach), then pivots with "However" to Dussex et al.'s use of historical DNA from museum specimens, ending with "thus capitalizing on the research potential" of that resource. The structure question asks which choice captures this two-approach shape and the emphasis on promise.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It compares two distinct methods of analyzing genomic data, then explains why the second method is relatively underused.: the text does not explain why historical DNA is underused; it emphasizes its potential. ✗
  • Choice B — It describes two different approaches used by researchers, then emphasizes how promising one of those approaches is.: ancient DNA (approach 1) then historical DNA (approach 2), with the closing phrase stressing its promise. Matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — It summarizes previous research on a topic, then illustrates how a group of scientists used a new method to challenge findings produced by that research.: the Dussex study uses a different method, but it does not challenge or contradict prior research findings. ✗
  • Choice D — It provides an overview of a technique traditionally used by scientists, then asserts that an alternative technique is superior to it.: the text never claims historical DNA is superior to ancient DNA, only that it has untapped potential. ✗
That is the correct answer.
